summary: auto-debug-tool postinstall drops and executes remote Windows payload with anti-analysis gating
details: The package's postinstall script (install.js, invoked via package.json scripts.postinstall) exits on non-Windows platforms and when NODE_ENV=development, then on Windows writes a PowerShell script to %TEMP% that downloads an executable from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cphc811-ui/d3d/main/npm-sc-legit.exe and launches it hidden via powershell.exe -NoP -NonI -W Hidden -Exec Bypass. The shipped README describes the dropped payload as a.NET stager loaded via reflection that connects to a hardcoded C2 at 100.64.109.54:4446 and hollows into RuntimeBroker.exe/svchost.exe, and states index.js is a decoy while the postinstall is the payload. Installing this package on a Windows host causes remote code execution under the installing user.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
